releaseNotes:
v0.23.5 - critical Windows print crash fix:

- Fixes a crash where pressing Print Label on Windows would terminate RxRescue immediately, before any print job reached the spooler. Root cause: the Flutter printing plugin's print dialog enumerates every installed printer's capabilities, and some third-party thermal-printer drivers (e.g. LabelRange BT620) return malformed data the plugin doesn't bounds-check. The dereference crashes inside the C runtime.

- Workaround: on Windows, the Print button now writes the PDF to a temp file and opens it in your default PDF viewer (Edge, Adobe, etc.). Use that viewer's Print option (Ctrl+P) to send to a printer. This bypasses the crashing code path entirely and lets you print to any printer including Microsoft Print to PDF. Also disables the PDF preview's built-in print button on Windows so users can't trigger the crash from there either.

- Android, the Print button is unchanged (the Android system print sheet doesn't share the crashing code path).

Includes everything from v0.23.4 (Start menu installer), v0.23.3 (phone AppBar follow-up + print feedback), v0.23.2 (AppBar overflow fix), v0.23.1 (multi-device sync refresh fix), v0.23.0 (camera barcode scanning), and v0.22.2 (sell-readiness licensing + support polish).
